_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier delta from L8 keeper to L9 'Heavy Plate + Closed Visor + First Gold Accents + Taller Pauldrons'. Two-image input: image_urls[0] = L8 v3 keeper (content reference), image_urls[1] = L1_Base.png (scale/framing/pose anchor only). Cloth tassets stay (steel fauld conversion is L9.5).
_meta.input_image_content: UnitProgression/Archer/out/v3/L8/composite/archer_L8_v<keeper>_composited.png (v3 chain, user picks keeper at runtime)
_meta.level_beat_doc: UnitProgression/Archer/Archer_Progression_Plan.md (L9 — Heavy Plate; revised 2026-05-07 per AD FeedbackV0)
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chain (single-input chained, locked 2026-05-07) — previous keeper composite is the sole input. No L1_Base scale anchor.
history: v1-v_visor_chain (2026-05-04 / 05): see prior history — closed visor, gambeson V-wedge at throat, gold belt buckle, gilt bow tips. Rivets at pauldron seam pulled forward to L8 in v_visor_chain.

v2_detail_economy (2026-05-07): MAJOR REWRITE per AD FeedbackV0 (CLAUDE.md Part 3 §23). (a) ADDED 'pauldrons grow taller' as a NEW L9 beat — the upper cap dome extends upward and outward, making the overall pauldron silhouette taller and more substantial. Volume bump replaces the surface-rivet beat that AD explicitly dropped. (b) STRENGTHENED gold rendering language for both the gold belt buckle and gilt bow limb tips per CLAUDE.md Part 2 §I (saturated buttery-yellow base + soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow + deeper warm shadow on underside reading honey-orange — addresses Infantry-L8 first-gold soft-fail pattern). (c) PRESERVE block updated to reflect v3 L8 state: red MANTLE at shoulders (not red cloth coif/scarf at back of neck); FULL-arm scale mail visible at bicep+elbow between pauldron lower lame and vambrace upper edge (no bicep gap); FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown bow with brass grip-end bindings + brass tip caps (NO thin gold inner-curve inlay — dropped at L6 in v3); NO scale-mail coif at neck (retired at L7); NO dagger sheath (retired at L8); NO rivets at the pauldron seam (clean steel-on-steel from L8). (d) Updated paths to v3 chain. Adds count 4 → 5 (added pauldron volume-bump beat).

v3_single_input (2026-05-07): Dropped L1_Base from image_urls per locked single-input chained rule (CLAUDE.md Part 1 §B revised). Stripped 'SECOND input image' / scale-anchor language from prompt body and FRAMING block heading. Sole input is the previous composited keeper. urls_before=2, urls_after=1, body_chars_removed=47.

v4_dagger_restored (2026-05-08): Per AD feedback — dagger sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip carries through L8 (was retired in v2). EDIT GOAL preamble updated from 'brass-buckle wider waist belt — clean, no dagger' to '...with dark-wood dagger in a leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip'. PRESERVE block adds dagger back.

v5_boot_progression (2026-05-08): Per AD feedback — boots get a plate-only progression. STEEL TOE CAP moved earlier to L6 (first plate on boot, mid-chain). At L9 the boot adds STEEL HEEL CAP + STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND — heel cap matches the L6 toe cap; ankle guard is a thin curved steel band wrapping the boot at the ankle just above the leather cuff. Plate accumulation, no surface ornament (no eyelets / lacing / trim bands). Adds count 5 → 6.

adds.title: CLOSED FULL-FACE STEEL VISOR
adds.summary: extends the L8 half-visor downward into a FULL closed visor that covers the FACE FULLY from forehead to chin.

adds.title: RED GAMBESON V-WEDGE AT THE THROAT (CENTER-FRONT)
adds.summary: a CLEAR crimson-red triangular cloth wedge becomes visible at the throat, sitting center-front BETWEEN the lower edge of the closed visor (above) and the upper edge of the steel breastplate (below).

adds.title: BUTTERY-GOLD RECTANGULAR BELT BUCKLE REPLACES BRASS
adds.summary: the brass rectangular belt buckle from the input image is replaced by a polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD rectangular buckle, slightly larger than the prior buckle (about 1.

adds.title: GILT BOW LIMB TIPS (OUTER ~15% OF EACH LIMB)
adds.summary: the outermost ~15% of EACH bow limb (both the upper limb tip and the lower limb tip) is now painted in polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD instead of warm wood-brown.

adds.title: PAULDRONS GROW TALLER
adds.summary: VOLUME BUMP — both two-plate steel pauldrons from the input image grow TALLER and MORE SUBSTANTIAL via a volume bump on the upper cap.

adds.title: STEEL HEEL CAP + STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND ON EACH BOOT
adds.summary: Plate accumulation on the boots — adds a polished steel heel cap (matches the L6 steel toe cap) on the back of each heel + a thin curved steel ankle guard band wrapping each boot horizontally at the ankle just above the leather cuff. The L6 steel toe cap carries through unchanged. Three steel plates total now: toe / heel / ankle band. Plate-only — no eyelets, no lacing, no trim bands.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Take the archer in the input image (conical steel helm with gold trim band and steel half-visor covering the upper face with two horizontal eye slits, lower face still visible, polished steel breastplate, steel vambraces with thin gold trim at the wrist edge, layered two-plate steel pauldrons with clean steel-on-steel seam between upper cap and lower lame, FULL-ARM scale mail covering bicep through elbow through forearm with vambrace on top, mustard tunic with red panels visible as a thin compressed livery band between breastplate and the wider leather waist belt, brass-buckle wider waist belt with dark-wood dagger in a leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip (carries through from L3), alternating crimson-red and mustard-yellow CLOTH TASSETS at hip down to mid-thigh, leather greaves below the knee, brown boots, brown back-quiver with white-feather red-tip arrows, FULL LONGBOW proportions wood-brown bow with brass grip-end bindings + brass tip caps, red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of neck, crimson cape, olive trousers) and apply SIX clearly-visible upgrades to produce the 'Heavy Plate + Closed Visor + First Gold Accents + Taller Pauldrons + Boot Heel Cap + Ankle Guard' variant. The character is the same archer in the same pose at the same scale and framing — only the six adds change. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) CLOSED FULL-FACE STEEL VISOR — extends the L8 half-visor downward into a FULL closed visor that covers the FACE FULLY from forehead to chin. The L8 half-visor (which covered the upper face from helm rim down to the upper cheekbones with two horizontal eye slits) is now extended downward with an additional steel plate that covers the lower cheeks, the nose, the mouth, the jawline, and the chin. The two original horizontal eye slits at the upper face MERGE into a SINGLE HORIZONTAL RECESSED EYE SLIT at eye level (about half-inch tall, running across the visor's width). The visor flares slightly outward at the cheekbone level and tucks back inward toward the jaw, forming a soft elongated diamond profile when viewed in three-quarter. Same polished cool silver-gray steel material as the helm body, with a soft top-down highlight on the upper visor surface and a warm-brown reflected shadow on the lower edge. NO gold trim on the visor itself. The face from the input (eyes, nose, mouth, jawline, eyebrows) is now FULLY CONCEALED — no skin visible above the breastplate other than the side of the neck where the throat and the red mantle show. The conical helm body shape, the gold trim band on the helm, and the red mantle at the shoulders all carry through. (2) RED GAMBESON V-WEDGE AT THE THROAT (CENTER-FRONT) — a CLEAR crimson-red triangular cloth wedge becomes visible at the throat, sitting center-front BETWEEN the lower edge of the closed visor (above) and the upper edge of the steel breastplate (below). The wedge is roughly 2 inches tall and 2 inches wide at its top edge, tapering down to a point at the upper edge of the breastplate. The cloth is the same crimson-red as the cape, with subtle painted vertical fold lines reading as gambeson fabric. CENTER-FRONT placement (NOT inner shoulder) — the wedge sits visibly at the throat where the chest opens between the breastplate's collarbone curves. Reads as the gambeson tunic still worn under the plate, peeking through at the open neckline. (3) BUTTERY-GOLD RECTANGULAR BELT BUCKLE REPLACES BRASS — the brass rectangular belt buckle from the input image is replaced by a polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD rectangular buckle, slightly larger than the prior buckle (about 1.25x), in the same rectangular silhouette and same center-front placement on the wider leather belt. GOLD RENDERING: the buckle must read as POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the surrounding warm-brown leather, distinctly more yellow than brass, NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base across the buckle's full surface; (ii) soft top-down highlight reading almost white-yellow at the upper edge; (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside reading honey-orange. The buckle must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. NO engraving, NO gemstones, NO embossing — plain polished gold rectangle. The wider warm-brown leather belt itself remains unchanged. (4) GILT BOW LIMB TIPS (OUTER ~15% OF EACH LIMB) — the outermost ~15% of EACH bow limb (both the upper limb tip and the lower limb tip) is now painted in polished BUTTERY YELLOW GOLD instead of warm wood-brown. The gold zone starts at the brass tip cap (which now blends into the gold limb tip area as a continuous gilt section) and extends inward along about the outermost one-sixth of each limb's length. The transition from gold (outer) to wood-brown (inner remainder of the limb) is a soft gradient with a clean visible boundary. Same gold rendering treatment as the belt buckle — saturated buttery yellow base + top-down highlight + warm shadow on the underside, immediately readable as gold at thumbnail scale. The dark-brown leather grip wrap at the central handle remains unchanged; the brass binding rings at each end of the leather grip remain unchanged. (5) PAULDRONS GROW TALLER — VOLUME BUMP — both two-plate steel pauldrons from the input image grow TALLER and MORE SUBSTANTIAL via a volume bump on the upper cap. The upper dome cap of each pauldron extends upward and outward by approximately 1 inch — the dome is taller, more rounded at the top, and wider at the shoulder line — making the overall pauldron silhouette read as more massive at thumbnail scale. The lower lame from the input image stays in place at the upper bicep. The clean steel-on-steel seam between the (now-taller) upper cap and the lower lame remains as a thin shadow line, with NO rivets, NO brass dots, NO gold trim, NO decorative beading along the seam. Both plates remain the same polished cool silver-gray steel as before, with bright leading-edge highlights on the new taller upper cap dome. This is a VOLUME BUMP — the pauldron mass grows; no surface ornament is added. (6) STEEL HEEL CAP + STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND ON EACH BOOT — the boots gain additional plate coverage at the heavy-plate level. (a) Add a POLISHED COOL SILVER-GRAY STEEL HEEL CAP wrapping the back of each heel — a small curved steel plate matching the L6 toe cap's silver polish, covering the heel area with a clean upper edge against the warm-brown leather body. (b) Add a STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND wrapping each boot horizontally at the ANKLE — a thin curved steel band sitting just above the leather cuff, fully wrapping the ankle in polished cool silver-gray steel about 0.6 inches tall, with a clean upper edge and a clean lower edge against the leather. The L6 STEEL TOE CAP at the front of each boot CARRIES THROUGH UNCHANGED. Three steel plates total on each boot now: TOE CAP (front, from L6) + HEEL CAP (back, new) + ANKLE GUARD BAND (horizontal at ankle, new). PLATE accumulation only — NO ankle plate edge filigree, NO rivets along the band, NO surface ornament, NO trim line wrapping the leather cuff, NO eyelets, NO lacing detail. The boot's leather body remains chunky warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff visible BETWEEN the new ankle guard band above and the leather body below. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== the conical steel helm body shape (only the FACE is now closed by the new full visor extension; the helm body itself, the gold trim band on the helm, are unchanged); the red CLOTH MANTLE at the shoulders / back of the neck (the crimson-red mantle carried over from the input image, visible at the back of neck behind the closed visor's lower edge); the polished steel breastplate covering the upper chest from collarbones to mid-torso (only the new red throat wedge appears immediately above its upper edge); both layered two-plate steel pauldrons (upper dome cap + lower secondary lame, with the NEW taller upper cap per add 5 — but the lower lame is unchanged, the seam remains clean steel-on-steel with no rivets); both steel vambraces from wrist to just below the elbow with longitudinal seam visible on the outer side, with the existing thin BUTTERY GOLD trim line at each wrist edge; the FULL-ARM scale mail covering each arm from deltoid down to wrist (chunky overlapping cool silver-gray scale plates per the established scale-mail visual spec) — visible at the bicep + elbow between the (now-taller) pauldron's lower lame above and the steel vambrace's upper edge below (NO bare-skin gap on either arm); both hands BARE peachy-tan skin (no gloves, no gauntlets); the FULL LONGBOW held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height — wood-brown limb body (only the outer ~15% tips are now gold per add 4), dark-brown leather grip wrap, brass binding rings at each end of the grip, brass tip caps blending into the new gold tip area; the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver slung diagonally across the back over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, three to four arrows visible with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the crimson-red knee-length cape attached at both shoulders falling with soft painted vertical folds; the wider warm-brown leather waist belt (only the buckle changes from brass to gold per add 3); the dark-wood dagger in its leather sheath at the anatomical-RIGHT hip with the brass capstone on the handle (carries through unchanged — hangs from the wider belt above/in-front-of the tassets); the alternating crimson-red and mustard-yellow CLOTH TASSETS at the hip — distinct vertical cloth strips alternating R-Y-R-Y-R across the front and around the sides, hanging from the wider belt to mid-thigh — UNCHANGED from the input image (the steel fauld conversion arrives at the next intermediate level); the mustard-yellow tunic with the red center panel still visible as a compressed livery band between the breastplate's lower edge and the top of the wider belt (carried unchanged); the olive / muted forest-green knee-length cloth trousers visible above the greaves where the tassets stop; the warm-brown leather wrap-style greaves on the lower legs from boot cuff to just below the knee with single cross-strap binding; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with the darker folded ankle cuff (carries through with the L6 STEEL TOE CAP at the front toe of each boot unchanged; the NEW L9 steel heel cap + steel ankle guard band per add 6 are added on top — three steel plates total: toe / heel / ankle). ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately 15-25 degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE BOW ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) hangs DOWN at his side with the elbow only slightly bent, the bow held VERTICALLY at HIP height directly in front of the hip with the hand gripping the bow's central leather grip wrap. THE DRAW ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is bent sharply at the elbow at roughly 90 degrees, with the FOREARM HELD HORIZONTALLY ACROSS THE CHEST at upper-chest / sternum level (parallel to the ground). The vambraced hand sits at the CENTER OF THE CHEST area in front of the steel breastplate, slightly toward the bow side, in a TIGHT CLOSED FIST shape with the KNUCKLES facing OUTWARD toward the viewer. The fist does NOT touch the bow, the bowstring, or the breastplate. Calm balanced HERO STANCE; NOT an active draw, NOT a nocking motion, NOT a reach. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 75% of canvas vertical height (~765px tall) and approximately 57% of canvas horizontal width (~580px wide). TOP of helm at ~13.5% from top of canvas (~138px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at ~12% from bottom of canvas (~120px below). Character roughly vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. The taller pauldron upper caps may push slightly above the previous shoulder line but should not crowd the helm or push the canvas top margin — the helm apex remains the highest point of the silhouette. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning under the key light. Interior linework soft and value-driven, never hard black. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT keep any face visible above the breastplate — the closed visor FULLY conceals eyes, nose, mouth, jawline, eyebrows, and any forehead skin; only the side of the neck (where the red mantle and the cape attachment sit) shows skin tone or red cloth; DO NOT make the visor eye slit large or open enough to see the eyes through it — it is a thin recessed slit only; DO NOT add a finial, spike, plume, feather, crest, gold spike, or any apex ornament on the helm at this level (the gold finial arrives at a later level); DO NOT reshape the helm body taller or more pointed; DO NOT replace the cloth tassets with steel plates, steel faulds, or any metal skirt — the cloth tassets stay UNCHANGED in this step; DO NOT change the alternating red/yellow color sequence of the cloth tassets; DO NOT add gold trim to the breastplate edges, vambrace cuffs (other than the existing wrist-edge trim), or pauldron edges; DO NOT add a red gambeson wedge at the inner shoulder where the pauldron meets the body — the red wedge is at the THROAT CENTER-FRONT only; DO NOT make the red throat wedge larger than about 2x2 inches; DO NOT replace the red throat wedge with mustard yellow or any other color — it is crimson red only; DO NOT extend the gilt bow tips past the outer ~15% of each limb; DO NOT make the bow fully gold at this level; DO NOT add gold trim, gold band, gold rim, or any gold on the breastplate, vambraces (other than the existing wrist-edge trim), pauldrons, or helm body beyond the existing helm trim band, the new belt buckle, and the new bow tip gilt; DO NOT add brass rivets, brass studs, gold dots, or any seam decoration along the pauldron seam — the seam is a CLEAN STEEL-ON-STEEL shadow line; DO NOT add a third plate / lame to the pauldrons — exactly TWO stacked plates (the upper cap is now taller per add 5 + the unchanged lower lame); DO NOT make the pauldrons taller via additional smaller plates stacked on top — the volume bump is on the EXISTING upper cap (the cap dome grows); DO NOT add full metal sabatons or plate boots — only the small polished STEEL HEEL CAP and STEEL ANKLE GUARD BAND added per add 6, on top of the carried-through L6 STEEL TOE CAP; the rest of the boot stays warm-brown leather with the darker folded ankle cuff; DO NOT add boot eyelets, boot lacing strings, brass cuff trim bands, or any surface trim — boot beats are PLATE accumulation only; DO NOT add gauntlets, plate gloves, or finger armor — both hands remain BARE peachy-tan skin; DO NOT use FINE CHAINMAIL RING MESH, interlocking metal rings, fish-scale fine texture, or scale that reads as small dots — the visible scale mail at the bicep + elbow region between pauldron and vambrace is CHUNKY OVERLAPPING ROUNDED SCALES per the established visual spec; DO NOT shrink the scale mail back to short-sleeve at deltoid + upper bicep — at this level the scale mail is FULL-arm coverage carried over from the previous level; DO NOT leave a bare-skin band at the bicep; DO NOT swap the hand holding the bow; DO NOT shorten the bow back from full longbow proportions; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the cape color, length, or drape; DO NOT change the bow position; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors (white feather + red tips); DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background or add scenery; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
